teams.title: Platform
teams.subtitle: Control Center
teams.description: Owns platform guardrails, validator and deploy health, reference quality control, year and multi-year planning, and the efficiency question of what should be taught versus omitted.

roadmaps.label: Platform roadmap
roadmaps.updated: 2026-04-24 after completing Sprint P1.3 internal dashboard MVP
roadmaps.summary: Sprint 0.5 is signed off for Part A textbook/book production, Sprint P1.1 has proven the first Book 1 companion paragraph path, and Sprint P1.2 has technically proven the pattern on a second Book 1 companion paragraph.

teams.title: Lessen
teams.subtitle: Effective Teaching
teams.description: Owns front-end quality, lesson usability, differentiation, games, visuals, companion material quality, and turning technically valid material into effective teaching.

roadmaps.label: Lessen roadmap
roadmaps.updated: 2026-04-24 after companion pilot review, layout/UI reprioritization, visual-variant direction, L1.3A-C split, and L1.3B platform integration (news variant system, symmetry validator, game-visuals decision)

sprints.name: Green Gate Handoff
sprints.currentState: Book 1 Part A and the platform/book health routine are green.

sprints.name: First Companion Technical Pilot
sprints.currentState: `1.1.1` passes the current complete technical gate.

sprints.name: Second Companion Technical Probe
sprints.currentState: `1.1.2` proved the technical pattern can repeat; probe materials were removed for didactic rebuild.

sprints.name: Basic HTML Layout And Front-End Usability
sprints.currentState: Pilot run completed for `1.1.1` `uitleg voorkennis`, `uitleg vaardigheden`, and `begeleide inoefening`; needs human usability sign-off.

sprints.name: Companion SVGs And Light/Dark Visual Variants
sprints.currentState: Proper SVG/variant workflow is running for `1.1.1`; game/interactive visual slots still need a decision.

sprints.name: PowerPoint Presentation Improvement
sprints.currentState: `1.1.1` PPTX regenerated with adapted slide visuals and LibreOffice roundtrip; needs presentation-quality review.

sprints.name: Post-Layout Scaling Decision
sprints.currentState: Decide whether to expand after L1.3A-C are platform-integrated, regenerated, and reviewed.

sprints.currentState: Teacher-facing polish continues under the Book 1 health gate.

teams.title: Innovation
teams.subtitle: Collab Experiments
teams.description: Owns controlled experiments in the collab worktrees: better presentations, web-native lesson media, narration, and future ideas before adoption into production workflows.

roadmaps.label: Innovation platform roadmap
roadmaps.scope: collab work 3 innovation track for presentations, web-native lesson media, and later experiments
roadmaps.summary: The Innovation Team owns controlled experiments that may break the mold before they become production workflow. This track is allowed to take more risk than normal material production, but the risk stays inside the collab setup and becomes useful only when the learning can be turned into platform workflows, reusable builders, or clear product decisions.

sprints.name: Charter and baseline
sprints.currentState: What does "better PowerPoint" mean for one real lesson?
sprints.exitGate: One baseline presentation is selected, inspected, and scored against a short rubric.

sprints.name: First excellent presentation
sprints.currentState: Can we make one teacher-ready PowerPoint that is clearly better than the current pattern?
sprints.exitGate: One non-graph presentation is approved as the reference standard.

sprints.name: Presentation production loop
sprints.currentState: Can the improved standard be reproduced without heroic one-off work?
sprints.exitGate: Builder, checklist, render review, and QA notes are repeatable for a second presentation.

sprints.name: Graph presentations MVP
sprints.currentState: Can we build graph-heavy PowerPoints that are mathematically correct and visually calm?
sprints.exitGate: One graph-heavy presentation passes visual, economic, and geometry checks.

sprints.name: Graph scale-up
sprints.currentState: Can graph-heavy presentation production become a reliable pattern?
sprints.exitGate: Two more graph-heavy presentations are produced using the same graph pipeline.

sprints.name: Web-native presentation prototype
sprints.currentState: Can a presentation live naturally inside the lesson website instead of feeling like embedded Office?
sprints.exitGate: One approved presentation is rendered as a web-native lesson experience.

sprints.name: Web integration polish
sprints.currentState: Can web-native presentations work for students and teachers across devices?
sprints.exitGate: Navigation, responsive layout, assets, and teacher use case are validated in browser.

sprints.name: Speaker notes to speech prototype
sprints.currentState: Can speaker notes become a useful narrated experience?
sprints.exitGate: One short presentation has generated narration aligned to slides.

sprints.name: Video-like lesson experience
sprints.currentState: Can slides, notes, narration, and pacing create a coherent video-like flow?
sprints.exitGate: One complete prototype exports or plays as a narrated lesson sequence.

sprints.name: Innovation backlog reset
sprints.currentState: What should the innovation team attack next?
sprints.exitGate: Backlog is ranked by educational value, technical risk, and reusability.

roadmaps.label: Innovation output roadmap
roadmaps.scope: collab work 3 innovation track for presentations, web-native lesson media, and later experiments
roadmaps.summary: The Innovation Team owns controlled experiments that may break the mold before they become production workflow. This track is allowed to take more risk than normal material production, but the risk stays inside the collab setup and becomes useful only when the learning can be turned into platform workflows, reusable builders, or clear product decisions.

sprints.name: Rubric seed from last strong presentation
sprints.currentState: Which qualities made the last presentation pretty good, and where is it still not ready?
sprints.exitGate: A first rubric draft is created from the last strong presentation, with known gaps recorded.

sprints.name: First graph-heavy presentation
sprints.currentState: Can the next chapter's graph paragraph become a strong PowerPoint without losing economic precision?
sprints.exitGate: One graph-heavy presentation draft exists and has been reviewed for graph correctness, readability, and flow.

sprints.name: Flowchart presentation prototype
sprints.currentState: Can a presentation lean on very well-formed flowcharts as the main explanation structure?
sprints.exitGate: One flowchart-led presentation draft exists and has been reviewed for clarity, sequencing, and visual discipline.

sprints.name: Rubric and test revision
sprints.currentState: What must the rubric and tests learn from the graph and flowchart prototypes?
sprints.exitGate: The rubric, checks, and acceptance gates are revised using evidence from three presentation types.

sprints.name: Presentation production loop
sprints.currentState: Can the improved standard be reproduced without heroic one-off work?
sprints.exitGate: Builder, checklist, render review, and QA notes are repeatable for another presentation.

sprints.name: Web-native presentation prototype
sprints.currentState: Can a presentation live naturally inside the lesson website instead of feeling like embedded Office?
sprints.exitGate: One approved presentation is rendered as a web-native lesson experience.

sprints.name: Web integration polish
sprints.currentState: Can web-native presentations work for students and teachers across devices?
sprints.exitGate: Navigation, responsive layout, assets, and teacher use case are validated in browser.

sprints.name: Speaker notes to speech prototype
sprints.currentState: Can speaker notes become a useful narrated experience?
sprints.exitGate: One short presentation has generated narration aligned to slides.

sprints.name: Video-like lesson experience
sprints.currentState: Can slides, notes, narration, and pacing create a coherent video-like flow?
sprints.exitGate: One complete prototype exports or plays as a narrated lesson sequence.

sprints.name: Innovation backlog reset
sprints.currentState: What should the innovation team attack next?
sprints.exitGate: Backlog is ranked by educational value, technical risk, and reusability.

issueCategories.label: Inspection And Accountability
issueCategories.description: Evidence, traceability, coverage, and proof that materials meet school, inspection, and exam-program expectations.

issueCategories.label: Reference Quality
issueCategories.description: Correctness, freshness, deduplication, source validity, unit quality, term quality, and exam-code links.

issueCategories.label: Didactic Quality
issueCategories.description: Whether the right skills, complexes, goals, sequence, cognitive load, and misconceptions are handled efficiently.

issueCategories.label: Assessment And Exam Fit
issueCategories.description: Alignment with CvTE-style demands, question types, answer models, target skills, and exam realism.

issueCategories.label: Student And Teacher Experience
issueCategories.description: Readability, navigation, visuals, games, differentiation, teacher readiness, and classroom usability.

issueCategories.label: Platform Reliability
issueCategories.description: Repeatability and trustworthiness of generators, validators, deploys, links, tests, and reporting routines.

issueCategories.label: Production Readiness
issueCategories.description: Release and scaling blockers: incomplete artifacts, unresolved flags, manual steps, rebuild gaps, and known risks.

issueCategories.label: Innovation Transfer
issueCategories.description: Whether collab experiments are accepted, rejected, revised, or migrated into production workflows.

issues.title: News source URLs are not truly validated
Complete-mode quality refs can be stale
Review flags are not surfaced as scale blockers
Browser smoke checks are not part of the repeatable gate
Game-data tests are structural, not content-quality checks

greenGate.command: npm.cmd run check:book -- "..\4veco-lessen\Boek 1 - Grondslagen, vraag en aanbod"

companionPipeline.state: Complete technical proof; currently approved pilot surface for dashboard evidence.
Historical technical probe passed; test materials cleared and paragraph awaits didactic rebuild.
